title: Hybrid Retrieval, Score Calibration, and Rank Fusion · Runtime Lens: Rag Reciprocal Rank Fusion
summary: A hybrid system adds raw BM25 and cosine scores, gives missing results zero without defining meaning, and wins globally while exact-identifier queries regress. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the reciprocal rank fusion increment in hybrid retrieval and fusion evaluator. Learners progress through prediction,…
objectives: Implement reciprocal rank fusion with stable identities, rank constant, duplicate handling, missing candidates, and deterministic ties.
Avoid direct raw-score comparison across retrievers unless distributions and meaning are calibrated on target judgments.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: rag-reciprocal-rank-fusion — Implement reciprocal rank fusion with stable identities, rank constant, duplicate handling, missing candidates, and deterministic ties.
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-hybrid-fusion-2
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-hybrid-fusion-2.
# Case 7417: a regulated document deletion request.
# Operating constraint: record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace one exact identifier with a synonym and compare lexical, dense, and hybrid recall.
def worked_rag_hybrid_fusion_2(lexical_rank: int = 2, dense_rank: int = 5, rank_constant: int = 60, lexical_available: bool = True, dense_available: bool = True, calibrated: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "corpusworked2",
        "query2dekrow",
        "tenant7417",
        "indexworked27417",
        "prompt2dekrow7417",
        "evaluation7417worked2",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if rank_constant <= 0 or lexical_rank < 1 or dense_rank < 1:
        return False, 0.0, "rank-input"
    if not lexical_available and not dense_available:
        return False, 0.0, "no-retriever"
    lexical_score = 1.0 / (rank_constant + lexical_rank) if lexical_available else 0.0
    dense_score = 1.0 / (rank_constant + dense_rank) if dense_available else 0.0
    fused = lexical_score + dense_score
    if not calibrated:
        return False, fused, "fusion-evaluation"
    assert fused > 0.0
    return True, round(fused, 6), "reciprocal-rank-fusion"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Implement reciprocal rank fusion with stable identities, rank constant, duplicate handling, missing candidates, and deterministic ties.

hints: For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Reciprocal Rank Fusion model · Theory Rag Reciprocal Rank Fusion, name the stakeholder, corpus, source, document, chunk, model, index, query, relevance, tenant, context, citation, evaluation, and transfer boundary, Rag Reciprocal Rank Fusion precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RAG and Python Defend the Rag Reciprocal Rank Fusion model · Theory Rag Reciprocal Rank Fusion, trace this concrete input and predict ingestion, normalization, retrieval, filter, fusion, reranking, context, claim, citation, abstention, latency, cost, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory RAG engineering team handles case 5017 during a regulated document deletion request. Build deterministic Python evidence to fuse lexical and dense candidates without comparing uncalibrated raw scores; keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence; then change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
